goal: Add v0.1-rc6 patch spec file with corrections — fix field-name and ancestry-correctness bugs in the previously-uncommitted design doc

summary.title: Add rc6 phase-1 eligibility patch spec with field-name + ancestry fixes
summary.what: Lands the previously-uncommitted v0.1-rc6 patch design doc (docs_for_ai/mainline-spec-v0.1-rc6-patch.md) as a tracked file. While doing so, fixes four classes of bug in the spec text: (1) Patch 1 rule 4 referenced StatusEvidence.SupersededBy but the actual domain field is SupersededByIntent; (2) Patch 1 rule 5 referenced a nonexistent candidate.Supersedes / remote.Supersedes field — rewrote to detect direct supersede via the inverse direction (the only direction domain stores); (3) Patch 2 used `git rev-list <commit> --first-parent` for the ancestor set, which silently misses commits brought in by merge commits second-parent side, letting manually-pinned never-merged commits leak through the eligibility filter — switched to plain `git rev-list <commit>`; (4) Patch 5 §10.X spec text had matching field-name and rule-text bugs. Added an implementation-status note at the top so the next reader knows rc6 is unimplemented as of v0.2.
summary.why: rc6 was the alpha-user-driven phase-1 correctness patch that introduced the candidate-eligibility framework. The doc was drafted but never committed and never implemented; it sat in the working tree referencing field names that did not match the current domain model (Supersedes / SupersededBy vs. SupersededByIntent) and contained a real correctness bug in the ancestor-set helper (--first-parent skipping merge-brought-in commits). When the next person comes back to implement rc6 they would chase ghost field names and ship a buggy ancestor filter; fix it now while the contradictions are still in working memory.
summary.user_goal: Have a clean, current, implementable design baseline for the next phase-1 iteration, rather than an aspirational doc that drifts further from reality each refactor.

decisions.point: Surgical fixes vs. full rewrite to current state
decisions.chose: Surgical: fix factual bugs, add status header, keep design forward-looking
decisions.rationale: rc6 is a design doc for work not yet done. Rewriting it to "reflect current state" would erase the design intent. Fixing the bugs that would mislead an implementer (wrong field names, wrong git invocation) preserves the doc as actionable while removing the traps.

decisions.point: Detect supersede direction via inverse only
decisions.chose: Use remote.StatusEvidence.SupersededByIntent == candidate.IntentID (and reverse) — no new Supersedes field needed
decisions.rationale: Adding a Supersedes inverse field to IntentView would be a domain-model change that should be its own decision, not a side-effect of fixing a spec doc. The inverse-direction check covers the same logical relationship using only existing data.

decisions.chose: Block-quoted note at the very top, before the existing header block
decisions.rationale: Anyone scanning the doc will see "this is unimplemented" before they invest in reading the algorithm — saves the misread of "this is current behaviour".

rejected.alternative: Implement rc6 in this PR alongside the spec fixes
rejected.reason: Would conflate two different decisions (fix doc accuracy vs. ship behaviour change). The spec also itself notes rc6 is a 4-day implementation; doing it here would slow reviewer feedback on each part.

rejected.alternative: Delete the rc6 doc as never-implemented dead weight
rejected.reason: rc6 captures real alpha-user feedback and a real phase-1 false-positive class. The design is sound; we want to preserve it for the next iteration, not throw it away.

rejected.alternative: Keep --first-parent in the ancestor set with a caveat
rejected.reason: Not a documentation question — --first-parent is wrong for the ancestor semantic the patch describes. Caveat would mean "the spec proposes a buggy implementation"; we just remove the bug.

risks: Some non-obvious tweak in the spec might still be wrong — the doc was reviewed against the v0.2 codebase but rc6 was originally written against rc5, and a subtle semantic drift may not be caught by field-name matching alone. Mitigated by the status header which signals "verify against current code when implementing".

The supersede-via-inverse rewrite assumes view-rebuild populates StatusEvidence.SupersededByIntent for both sides of a supersede pair. Spot-checked sync.go EventIntentSuperseded handler — only the superseded side gets SupersededByIntent set. The superseder side does NOT have its own Supersedes field. The rule as written checks both directions of the inverse, which is correct.
followups: Implement rc6 phase-1 eligibility filter (the actual 4-day work the spec describes).
Per spec Patch 4: add `mainline check --explain` for phase-1 decision visibility.
Per spec Patch 6: build the conflict-cases fixture set (followup-no-conflict, concurrent-real, stale-merged, abandoned-skipped, superseded-forwarded).
When rc6 lands, update the implementation-status header at the top of this doc to reflect what is shipped vs. still pending.

architectural_claims: rc6 phase-1 eligibility filter spec is preserved as a design doc with corrected field references against the current StatusEvidence domain model.
Ancestor set computation in the spec uses plain `git rev-list <commit>` (no --first-parent); first-parent-only would miss commits brought in via merge commits.
